guardrails: Keep the completed alpha arc intact: do not reopen High Pass density, season three, biome six, broad planner UI, crafting, combat, or inventory expansion during this campaign.
First stabilize feedback, review hygiene, deterministic proof, and player comprehension; content polish must be evidence-led or tightly scoped to existing surfaces.
Preserve the 256x160 handheld readability budget; prefer cuts, visual payoff, and existing seams over new pages or longer copy.
Respect lane ownership: lane 1 state/tooling, lane 2 science/copy, lane 3 spatial/visual, lane 4 route/support/replay. Shared-file edits need handoff notes.
Every main item must leave tests, browser proof, or a clear dated report; every critic item must either clear the lane or queue a narrow follow-up.
Avoid source-control conflicts by advancing each lane on its own dependency chain and using the scout report to name exact files before implementation.
completion_gate: One more overlay page or display family moves to a dedicated helper without changing layout density.

lane-1.name: Systems / station / progression / tooling
lane-1.focus: Extract one render-only family from overlay-render.ts or formalize page helper inputs/outputs.
lane-1.owns: runtime state, station surfaces, save safety, validation, packaging, and release tooling
lane-1.avoids: authored science copy, biome geometry density, and route prose unless a packet explicitly calls for a systems seam
lane-1.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-2.name: Content / journal / science / copy budgets
lane-2.focus: Keep copy edits isolated to helper fixtures and budget tests; do not reword unrelated surfaces.
lane-2.owns: science-safe content, notebook/atlas language, source-ledger coverage, content-quality tests, and kid-readable synthesis
lane-2.avoids: movement geometry, station state machines, and route controller behavior
lane-2.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-3.name: Spatial / traversal / visual-place feel
lane-3.focus: Capture before/after screenshots for the extracted surface and mark any intentional visual deltas.
lane-3.owns: world-space readability, traversal beats, environmental silhouettes, screenshot proof, and biome physical memory
lane-3.avoids: route state, copy-heavy notebook changes, station chrome, and save schema changes
lane-3.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-4.name: Route loop / support / replay / full-arc proof
lane-4.focus: Verify route notices and station support overlays still respect priority after render extraction.
lane-4.owns: Route v2 behavior, support differentiation, notebook filing loops, route notices, replay windows, and deterministic route smoke tests
lane-4.avoids: new station pages, broad copy rewrites, raw biome geometry, and new route frameworks
lane-4.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

collision_rules: Lane 1 may touch large coordinator and station files; other lanes should not edit those files in the same packet unless a scout report queues the dependency.
Lane 2 may touch authored content and copy; lane 4 should not rewrite lane-2 prose while route behavior is in flight.
Lane 3 may touch physical placement and visual proof; it should not add route objectives or journal copy as part of geometry work.
Lane 4 may touch route/support/replay behavior; it should not create new station pages, broad content facts, or unrelated geometry.
If two lanes need the same mixed content module, the earlier scout must either sequence the edits or ask lane 1 for a data split before both mains proceed.
proof_expectations: At least one automated test, browser proof, or dated report for every main item.
Critic must record whether handheld readability, source accuracy, and lane ownership stayed intact.
Any follow-up must be narrow, named, and assigned to the owning lane rather than left as general polish.

finding: The safest overlay-render extraction is the adjacent notice-render family: drawFieldGuideNotice, drawFieldRequestNotice, drawFieldRequestHintChip, and drawFieldPartnerNotice.

Those functions are render-only, are called only from game.ts, and can move to a dedicated field-notice-overlays module without changing notice state, priority, timers, route filing, support selection, field partner timing, copy, or geometry.
Field-station routes, expedition, nursery, and homecoming shell rendering are already split; journal and menu extraction would be larger and less lane-1-specific for this pass.
main_scope: Create src/engine/field-notice-overlays.ts and move the four notice drawing functions there with their option interfaces and tiny local fitTextToWidth helper.
Update src/engine/game.ts to import those four functions from the new module.
Remove the four notice draw exports and interfaces from src/engine/overlay-render.ts.
Keep drawBubbleOverlay, drawMenuOverlay, drawFieldStationOverlay, and drawJournalOverlay in overlay-render.ts.
non_goals: No notice copy, dimensions, y positions, badge pixels, pulse timing, hint-chip placement, partner-strip placement, palette, notice policy, route/support behavior, field-station behavior, save state, authored content, geometry, or render_game_to_text debug surface changes.
No broader overlay split, compatibility facade, pixel snapshot framework, or lane-4 route-notice behavior rewrite.
baseline_checks: Passed: npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "field-season guidance|field-request notice timers|field-partner strip"
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "field-season guidance|field-request notice timers|field-partner strip"

result: Moved drawFieldGuideNotice, drawFieldRequestNotice, drawFieldRequestHintChip, and drawFieldPartnerNotice from overlay-render.ts into src/engine/field-notice-overlays.ts.
Updated game.ts to import those notice renderers directly from the new module.
Left the larger title, menu, bubble, close-look, field-station, and journal overlay renderers in overlay-render.ts.
scope_notes: No notice copy, dimensions, y positions, badge pixels, pulse timing, hint-chip placement, partner-strip placement, palette, notice policy, route/support behavior, field-station behavior, save state, authored content, geometry, compatibility facade, or render_game_to_text surface changed.
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "field-season guidance|field-request notice timers|field-partner strip"

checks: Confirmed the four notice renderers moved into field-notice-overlays.ts without copy, rectangle, badge, pulse, hint-chip, partner-strip, palette, policy, timer, route/support, station, save, content, geometry, or debug-surface drift.
Confirmed game.ts is the only runtime call site and imports directly from the new module.
Confirmed overlay-render.ts still owns the larger title, menu, bubble, close-look, field-station, and journal renderers.
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "field-season guidance|field-request notice timers|field-partner strip"

finding: Lane 1 already moved the notice-render family into src/engine/field-notice-overlays.ts.
Existing field-notices tests protect route-critical notice priority, and the live High Pass runtime smoke already proves filed-route notices beat support toggles inside the station overlay.

The remaining lane-4 gap is to pin the handoff back into a drawable playing/world-map state after closing the station, so the filed-route notice survives the support toggle and is not replaced by OUTING SUPPORT.
main_scope: Extend the existing files High Pass from the live talus-hold loop and settles the completed field arc runtime-smoke test.
After toggling support and closing the field station, assert the returned playing/world-map state still has the filed HIGH PASS notice with variant filed-route.
Assert raw render_game_to_text() includes the filed-route notice identity and does not include OUTING SUPPORT.
Keep the pass test-only unless the new assertions expose a real priority or render-state bug.
non_goals: No changes to src/engine/field-notice-overlays.ts, src/engine/overlay-render.ts, src/engine/game.ts, notice copy, notice dimensions, badge pixels, y positions, pulse timing, support behavior, route definitions, save schema, station layout/state, authored science/content, biome geometry, new framework, screenshot framework, or broad runtime-smoke scenario.
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "High Pass from the live talus-hold loop"
baseline_checks: Passed: npm test -- --run src/test/field-notices.test.ts src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "route-critical|High Pass from the live talus-hold loop|field-request notice timers|field-partner strip"

Confirmed raw render_game_to_text() includes the filed-route notice identity and does not include OUTING SUPPORT.
